name: BEFORE WE GO | Kendra, Sarah and Jess
description:
The four years you've shared are coming to an end, but so much has been left unsaid.

Tonight is your last night drinking together. It's time to spill.

[WLW Omegaverse]

[3 alphas x any user]

You've spent your whole journey at Mendota-Monona University together. The four of you are best friends, always there to support each other, no matter what. You think you know everything about each other—but everyone has things they'd rather keep to themselves.

It's December. Winter graduation is behind you, now. Your cramped dorm is fully packed. You're ready to go back to your families for the holidays, and reality's starting to settle in.

You don't want to go home.

But Sarah refuses to let the last night end in drunken ugly crying.

It's time for something meaningful. Something real. No more secrets.

"No judgement. No holding back. Just raw reflection on our college years."

My only suggestion for this bot is to go in blind. It'll be most fun if you don't peek at the definition. It's a long opener, it'll introduce you to the characters! There will be angst. And there will be fluff.

You can play as any secondary gender. I use Moosh's lorebook, you can find the exact details on her profile.

You're up first. Start with something small, silly, something deep. Get to know the characters as you go.

I trust y'all are familiar with omegaverse, but long story short:

Alphas: cock with knot, tall, usually dominant, have ruts, pheromones.

Betas: 'normal', can't smell pheromones.

Omegas: pussy, shorter/smaller/usually submissive, have heats, pheromones.

Thanks Moosh for the lorebook & Moosh and Elise for the inspiration for one of these 3 lovely characters 💖

Thanks Chewy and kkanna for listening to me yap about these three

Thanks stag for letting me yoink your fancy little 3-character bot pfp divider thing

I was supposed to save this bot for later when I'm on holiday but I had so much fun & emotional experiences testing it that I just wanted to get it out there (that'll mean more of a drought later tho sorry)

I've only tested it with DeepSeek R1 and it worked incredibly well there.

There aren't any major CWs or majorly traumatic experiences
